title: 2 Kings: Judah's Last Days
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of 2 kings: judah's last days and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study 2 kings: judah's last days and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: Which king of Judah discovered the Book of the Law in the temple during repair work? Answer: Josiah.
acceptable_variants: Josiah son of Amon king of judah
the young king josiah who tore his robes on hearing the law

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The reforming king of Judah who discovered the Book of the Law in 2 Kings 22 and led a major covenant renewal; his reforms delayed Judah's judgment but could not reverse the accumulated sin
The deportation of Judah to Babylon in three stages: 605, 597, and 586 BC under Nebuchadnezzar; the temple was destroyed in 586 BC, fulfilling the covenant curses of Deuteronomy 28
